mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly lifting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate exceeding a 0.5 percentage point monthly increase signals an abrupt labor market shock that triggers risk-off equity repricing within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment spike, or the signal shows no predictive power over a 3-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer credit expansion accelerates while industrial production stagnates, it indicates demand-pull inflationary pressure that forces the Fed to tighten policy, compressing equity multiples over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term shows a negative coefficient in a multivariate regression but SPX forward returns remain positive in over 55% of observations, or the joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over 10 years.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained rise in the industrial production index above its 12-month moving average confirms robust manufacturing capacity expansion, which supports corporate profit growth and drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when INDPRO trades above its 12-month MA, or the signal generates a negative information coefficient over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sequential increase in real GDP growth rate signals expanding aggregate demand that directly boosts corporate revenue expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: The rolling correlation between quarterly GDP growth surprises and 5-day forward SPX returns remains below 0.10 for three consecutive years, or forward returns are negative in more than 50% of positive GDP growth months.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 0.25 percent, it marks the end of the zero-lower-bound environment, triggering a repricing of duration risk that depresses equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Equity forward returns remain positive in over 65% of instances following the rate threshold breach, or the signal's predictive rank IC drops below 0.02 across all post-2000 data.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates synchronized labor market and construction sector expansion, which amplifies corporate earnings visibility and drives positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to produce a positive information coefficient over a 10-year walk-forward test, or SPX quarterly returns are not significantly higher when both series expand simultaneously versus when only one expands.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of elevated consumer credit growth combined with rising unemployment, retail demand becomes fragile, causing a sharp equity drawdown over the next week as earnings revisions turn negative.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in more than 55% of triggered instances, or the interaction effect vanishes when controlling for VIX levels over a 3-year out-of-sample period.
